steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says 'Before that, the film he least enjoyed ______ the more recent classic *Slumdog Millionaire* (2008).' The phrase 'Before that' anchors the blank to a time earlier than another past event (watching The Maltese Falcon at last year's festival). Two past events in sequence require past perfect for the earlier one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — has been: present perfect — places the action in relation to now, not to the past anchor event. ✗
  • Choice B — had been: past perfect — correctly marks this as the earlier of two past events. ✓
  • Choice C — is: simple present — wrong tense entirely; the story is set in the past. ✗
  • Choice D — was being: past continuous — describes an ongoing past action, not a completed state, and doesn't signal the required time sequence. ✗
That is the correct answer.
